evidence_for: Minimum cluster share is 9.0%, so no segment is tiny.
evidence_against: Silhouette is 0.239 and Davies-Bouldin is 1.584, weaker than k=5.
recommendation: Not preferred; useful as a simpler fallback only.

evidence_for: Best baseline silhouette at 0.221; largest cluster share is 55.6%; mean seed ARI is 1.000.
evidence_against: Smallest cluster share is 7.9%, so one segment is relatively small and needs business interpretation.
recommendation: Still supported as the practical final candidate, with k=6 noted as the closest alternative.

evidence_for: Largest cluster share falls to 30.0% and seed stability is very high, with mean ARI 0.999.
evidence_against: Silhouette falls to 0.152 and Calinski-Harabasz is lower than k=5.
recommendation: Worth reviewing in the final discussion, but not enough evidence to replace k=5 now.
